nonGoals: Do NOT change production code (scorer.go, pipeline.go, domain types, etc.)
Do NOT create or modify language_tiers.go
Do NOT touch report generation or init_cmd.go
Do NOT add tests for unsupported languages or aggregate in this deliverable — those are in the next one
objective: Add regression guard and baseline tests: TestScore_SupportedLanguage_StillWorks and TestScore_DefaultsNotInjected
planText:
Add two test functions to internal/engine/scorer_test.go (package engine_test):

1. TestScore_SupportedLanguage_StillWorks — construct a Go Unit via domain.NewUnit (or equivalent factory) with realistic evidence (CodeMetrics with complexity > 0, coverage, lint pass, test pass). Call engine.CertifyUnit or engine.Score as the post-#25 API dictates. Assert the returned Grade is one of {A, A-, B+, B, C, D, F} (not 'N/A') and DimensionScores is non-empty with at least one dimension populated. This is a smoke test that the primary happy path still works after #25 refactoring.

2. TestScore_DefaultsNotInjected — construct a Unit (any language, including Go) and call Score/CertifyUnit. Iterate over the returned DimensionScores map and assert no dimension has value exactly 0.5 (the old default). Assert every dimension present has a value outside [0.49, 0.51] to catch near-defaults. This guards against accidental re-injection of hardcoded defaults.

Follow existing test conventions: use t.Run sub-tests, import engine_test package, use evidence.CodeMetrics{...}.ToEvidence() and policy.EvaluationResult{} as appropriate. Use the post-#25 API signatures (which may differ from the investigation snapshot).

nonGoals: Do NOT change production code (scorer.go, pipeline.go, domain types, etc.)
Do NOT create or modify language_tiers.go
Do NOT touch report generation or init_cmd.go
Do NOT duplicate the regression/baseline tests from deliverable 0
objective: Add unsupported-language and aggregate-skip tests: TestScore_UnsupportedLanguage_ReturnsNA, TestScore_UnmeasuredComplexity_NotScored, TestScore_AggregateSkipsUnsupported
planText:
Add three test functions to internal/engine/scorer_test.go (package engine_test):

1. TestScore_UnsupportedLanguage_ReturnsNA — construct a Unit with Language='Swift' (or any identifier not registered in the analysis package). Call the post-#25 Score/CertifyUnit API. Assert that the returned Grade equals the new GradeNA constant (or string 'N/A'), and that an Unsupported marker is true (if added to CertificationRecord). Assert that DimensionScores is either nil or an empty map — no fabricated dimension scores for unsupported languages.

2. TestScore_UnmeasuredComplexity_NotScored — construct a Unit whose language maps to TierGeneric (e.g. 'Ruby', 'Swift', or any unregistered language) with Complexity=0 (or absent from evidence). Call Score/CertifyUnit. Assert that NO dimension has a score of 0.95 (the 'excellent' tier that the old complexity<=5 branch would emit). Specifically: if maintainability dimension exists, it must not equal 0.95; if the language is truly unsupported, assert no dimensions at all. This guards the bug where zero complexity was mis-scored as excellent for languages that can't measure complexity.

3. TestScore_AggregateSkipsUnsupported — if the #25 changes added an aggregate function (e.g. AggregateScores or similar in scorer.go or a companion file), call it with a slice containing one supported Go unit and one unsupported Swift unit. Assert that the unsupported unit is excluded from the aggregated result (i.e., the result contains only the Go unit's contribution). If the aggregate function lives in a different package (e.g. workspace.AggregateCards), skip this test with t.Skip and a comment noting the package. If no aggregate function exists, write the test as t.Skip with a TODO referencing #25.

Follow existing test conventions in the file. Use the post-#25 API signatures.
